chunks.text: Kam Lau wins IEEE/RSE James Clerk Maxwell Medal - EECS at Berkeley Kam Lau (Photo: Caltech) EECS Professor Emeritus Kam-Yin Lau has won the IEEE/RSE James Clerk Maxwell Medal. The medal, named in honor of Scottish Physicist James Clerk Maxwell, recognizes groundbreaking contributions with exceptional impact on the development of electronics and electrical engineering, or related fields. The award consists of a gold medal, a bronze replica, a certificate, and an honorarium. Lau was honored “for spearheading high-speed semiconductor lasers and RF-over-Fiber Systems, enabling today’s wireline and wireless broadband access.” Categories Faculty Honors Industry Related Links IEEE/RSE James Clerk Maxwell Medal View Open Faculty Positions
